entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop (Pennisetum glaucum) studied for its root and shoot characteristics in sandy soils

entities.name: WET EXCAVATION METHOD
entities.description: A technique for the rapid removal of intact root systems of pearl millet from sandy soils

entities.name: SANDY SOIL
entities.description: The soil condition found in the Sahel region where the study was conducted

entities.name: SADORE
entities.description: A location in Niger where the pearl millet study was performed

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country where the research site Sadore is located

entities.name: SAHEL
entities.description: The region characterized by sandy soils where the study took place

entities.name: ROOT/SHOOT RELATIONS
entities.description: The study of the relationship between root and shoot growth and development

entities.name: SEMINAL ROOT LENGTH
entities.description: The length of the seminal root, which showed exponential growth in the first 10 days after emergence

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: The vertical growth of the plant, which increased linearly in the first 10 days after emergence

entities.name: ROOTING DEPTH
entities.description: The maximum depth reached by the roots of pearl millet, recorded as 168 cm

entities.name: ROOT AXES AND PRIMARY LATERALS
entities.description: The number of root axes and primary laterals, recorded as 172 per plant

entities.name: ROOT LENGTH
entities.description: The total length of the root system, which exceeded 5000 cm per plant by 75 days after emergence

entities.name: ROOT DRY MATTER
entities.description: The proportion of total dry matter accumulated in the roots, decreasing from 30% to less than 20% by maturity
